DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF ROXANNE JEN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND PRODUCTION DEMANDS; TO COMPEL PRODUCTION DOCUMENT; AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS: PLAINTIFFS' UNOPPOSED MOTIONS TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES SET ONE, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. WITHIN FOURTEEN (14) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER DEFENDANT WILL SERVE WITHOUT OBJECTIONS RESPONSES TO THE FORM AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND WILL PRODUCE THE DOCUMENTS REQUESTED. THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS IS THE AMOUNT OF $1,860.00 PAYMENT OF WHICH MUST BE MADE BY DEFENDANTS TO PLAINTIFFS WITHIN TEN (10) DAY FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER PREVIOUSLY FILED WITH THE MOTION WITH APPROPRIATE MODIFICATIONS OR, ALTERNATIVELY, PLAINTIFFS MAY EMAIL TO THE COURT AN NEW PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THE TENTATIVE RULING. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Status Conference Hearing
Off Calendar Oct-06-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being continued from Sep-22-2022 for continued hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of judgment.

Dept. 514; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:37:00 a.m. and concluding at 10:27:19 a.m.

Plaintiff Roxanne t. Jen represented by Donald J. Sweet, Esq. and Brett Ralston, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Michael P. Loney, an Indivdual not appearing.
Defendant Michael P. Loney, Trustee of the Michael P. Loney Revocable Living Trust dated April 21, 2006 not appearing.

Hearing on final judgment quieting title proceeds.
Court confers with counsel to determine damages sought by Plaintiff.

Court determines the sum of $723,483.00 is on deposit with the Court after the sale of Unit 1B.

Rental value of Unit 4C located at 139 S. Lake Merced Hills, San Francisco, CA 94132 is determined to be $3,500.00.

The matter is submitted.
Based on the summary of the case, comments made by Plaintiff's Counsel, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has proven each essential element of the complaint.

FINAL ORDER AND JUDGMENT

1. Title of the real property located at 139 South lake Merced Hills, Apartment 4C, San Francisco, CA 94132-2929 ("Apartment 4C") is vested in Plaintiff as Acting Trustee of the Cathering A. Loney Revocable Living Trust dated November 28, 2000. Plaintiff is the rightful holder of title to Apartment 4C and Defendants Michael P. Loney Revocable Living Trust dated April 21, 2006 have no estate, right, title or interest in Apartment 4C adverse to Plaintiff;

2. Plaintiff is awarded judgment against Defendants Michael P. Loney, an indivdual, and Michael P. Loney, Trustee of the Michael P. Loney Revocable Living Trust dated April 21, 2006 on Plaintiff's First Cause of Action for Ejectment;

3. Defendant Michael P. Loney, and all occupants therein shall immediately, fully, and completely vacate the real property located at 139 S. Lake Merced Hills, Apartment 4C, San Francisco, CA 94132-2929;

4. For the real property located at 139 S. Lake Merced Hills, Apartment 4C, San Francisco, CA 94132-2929, Plaintiff shall be entitled to immediate and exclusive possession against Defendant Michael P. Loney, and all occupants therein;

5. Plaintiff shall recover from costs of suit in the sum of $450.00 against Michael P. Loney, an individual and as Trustee of the Michael P. Loney Revocable Living Trust dated April 21, 2006;

6. Plaintiff shall recover an offset of Defendant's share of the $723,483.00 net sale proceeds from the sale of Apartment 1B. From the deposited funds of $723,483.00, Plaintiff shall recover one half of the deposited funds in the sum of $361,741.50, plus $1,860.00 in prior monetary sanctions, plus $45,388.33 for half of the advances to Apartment 1B, plus $128,100.00 in additional holdover damages for Apartment 4C, and $450.00 in costs of suit awarded hereinabove, for a total damages award to Plaintiff in the sum of $537,539.83.

7. Distribution of the funds presently deposited with the Court relative to this case shall be as follows:

A. To Plaintiff, Roxanne T. Jen, as Trustee of the Catherine A. Loney Revocable Trust dated November 28, 2000: $537,539.83

B. To Michael P. Loney as Trustee of the Michael P. Loney Revocable Living Trust dated April 21, 2006: $185,943.17

Proposed judgment signed as modified.

Notice Of Status Conference Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-22-2022 Continued To Oct-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being continued from 07/21/2022 for entry of judgment.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:51:21 a.m., pausing at 09:55:52 a.m., reconvening at 11:14:10 a.m. and concluding at 11:40:37 a.m.

Plaintiff Roxanne T. Jen, acting trustee of the Catherine A. Loney Revocable Living Trust dated November 28,2000 represented by Brett Ralston, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Micahel P. Loney not appearing.

Based on Plaintiff's CCP 585 default judgment request against Defendant, Plaintiff is seeking an order via the default judgment request to offset damages from Defendant's intended distribution by the Partition Referee. Plaintiff seeks $585,655.74 in monetary damages as requested in Apri, 2022, plus $4,200.00 each month for additional holdover damages.

Plaintiff is also seeking an order for possession of the remaining property subject to this action at 139 Lake Merced Hill #4C, San Francisco, CA 94132, in addition to the distribution offset at final order.

Plaintiff presents argument in support of it's request for ejectment of property.
Court determines the damand to vacate was served on Defendant Sep-20-2019. Rental damages are requested from this date to present.

Plaintiff must establish a reasonable rental value.
Court to determine whether ejectment can be in connection with quieting of title.
Court orders matter continued to Oct-06-2022 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 514 for further hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment and possession of the remaining property subject to this action at 139 Lake Merced Hill #4C, San Francisco, CA 94132.

Off Calendar Jul-21-2022 Continued To Sep-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Brett Ralston, Esq. 818-737-1090 RODNUNSKY & ASSOCIATES for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

Hearing is held. Mr. Ralston gives a brief overview to the Court re status of the property. The Court inquires how client is prejudiced if the matter is continued. Mr. Ralston informs the Court there is no prejudice to client except for additional holdover damages. The Court needs a clear picture and will continue the matter after the hearing of August 5, at 9:30 am in Dept. 501. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

STATUS CONFERENCE HEARING RE: SUBMISSION OF FINAL REPORT AND ENTRY OF FINAL JUDGMENT.

Motion For Order Confirming Sale Of Real Property And Opportunity For Overbid
Off Calendar Sep-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

David S. Rosenbaum; McDowall * Cotter, A.P.C.; 650-572-7933; appearing in person on behalf of partition referee Michael G. Kasolas.
Michael G. Kasolas; partition referee, appearing in person with their counsel of record.
Brett P. Ralston; Rodnunsky & Associatets; 650-285-5400; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiff Roxanne T. Jen.

Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR # 10619, raquel@transcriptlegal.com, 925-384-0003.

Michael Kasolas introduces the interested parties as follows:

Current buyers: Keith Truong and Vanny Seng.

Overbidders: Patricia Gray, Meredith Wong and Ashley Johnson, Pankaj Shah, Pamela Wilson and Timothy Wilson, Ritchie Jong and Pauline Tan.

Mr. Kasolas proceeds with the overbid process, with an opening bid starting at $798,500.00. The overbid process concludes, and the referee accepts overbidders Pamela Wilson and Timothy Wilson's increased offer of $955,000.00. The court finds that Notice of Sale was properly given and that there was no evidence presented that the proceedings were unfair. The court rules as follows:

REFEREE MICHAEL G. KASOLAS' MOTION FOR ORDER CONFIRMING SALE OF REAL PROPERTY AND OPPORTUNITY FOR OVERBID is granted.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR # 10619, raquel@transcriptlegal.com, 925-384-0003. (D501)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-16-2020 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-17-2020 Continued To Dec-21-20 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day having been assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Michael P. Loney, et al.
Dept: 514. Judge: Gerardo C Sandoval. Clerk: Lyssette Bareng. Not Reported. Parties waive the services of the Court Reporter for this afternoon's proceedings.
Donald J. Sweet, Esq. appeared with his associate Ariana Canalez for plaintiff Roxanne T. Jen.
Plaintiff Roxanne T. Jen was present, was placed under oath and testified.
Dan Tomczak, witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath but did not testify.
No appearance by defendants Michael P. Loney, an individual; Michael P. Loney, a Trustee of the Michael P. Loney Revocable Living Trust dated April 21, 2006.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Ejectment, for Partition by Sale by Right, and to Quiet Title filed on December 13, 2019.
Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents personally served on defendants Michael P. Loney, an individual; Michael P. Loney, a Trustee of the Michael P. Loney Revocable Living Trust dated April 21, 2006.
Answer to complaint by defendant Michael P. Loney was filed on February 25, 2020.
Default as to both defendants was entered on February 25, 2020.
DOE defendants have not been dismissed and will be dismissed at the next calling of the case.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial and Trial Order served on April 30, 2020 setting Trial for today, November 16, 2020 in Department 206, transferred to Department 514.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Roxanne T. Jen, and against the defaulted defendants Michael P. Loney, an individual; Michael P. Loney, a Trustee of the Michael P. Loney Revocable Living Trust dated April 21, 2006, Jointly and Severally, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $54,600.00.
Attorney Fees - RESERVED by the Court.
Costs - RESERVED by the Court.

Total - $54,600.00.

In addition, the Court GRANTED possession of the the Real Property located at 139 S. Lake Merced Hills, Apartment 4C, San Francisco, California 94132 on or before December 16, 2020.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Matter is CONTINUED to December 21, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. in Department 514 for further prove up on the Quiet Title Action, Partition Request and Referee Appointment on the Real Property located at 139 S. Lake Merced Hills, Apartment 1B, San Francisco, California 94132.
